title: Problem 1.3
statement_text: What is the infimum of fractional chromatic numbers of planar graphs without cycles of length 4 or 5?
context_text: The authors prove that every planar graph without cycles of length 4 or 5 is $(11:3)$-colorable, giving an upper bound of $\frac{11}{3}$ on the fractional chromatic number. They note the counterexample to Steinberg's conjecture from [4] is $(6:2)$-colorable, so the answer could be as low as $3$.
paper_title: Planar graphs without cycles of length 4 or 5 are (11:3)-colorable

title: Informal conjecture on optimality of 11/3
statement_text: It is not clear whether the constant $\frac{11}{3}$ from Theorem 1.1 is the best possible, and we suspect this is not the case.
context_text: Immediately after stating Problem 1.3, the authors express their belief that $\frac{11}{3}$ is not the tight infimum of fractional chromatic numbers for planar graphs without cycles of length 4 or 5, motivating the open problem.
paper_title: Planar graphs without cycles of length 4 or 5 are (11:3)-colorable
